name: Sentry
tagline: Errors, traces, and root-cause analysis for debugging agents.
description: Sentry's hosted MCP server gives agents direct access to issues, events, releases, and Seer root-cause runs — the context a coding agent needs to fix what's actually broken in production.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0
methods.note: The hosted MCP server authenticates via OAuth in supporting clients.

methods.label: Auth token
methods.note: Organization or personal tokens for the REST API and CI.

auth.guide:
**MCP with OAuth (easiest):** add `https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp` in an OAuth-capable client and approve access to your org.

```bash
claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp
```

**Auth tokens (REST):** create an organization token under [Settings → Auth Tokens](https://sentry.io/orgredirect/organizations/:orgslug/settings/auth-tokens/) — or a personal token under User settings — and send it as a bearer header:

```bash
curl https://sentry.io/api/0/projects/ \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN"
```

Organization tokens are scoped at creation; give debugging agents read-only event/issue scopes unless they need to resolve or assign.
